quickId: Two parallel dark stripes on pronotum
Nymphs nearly black with a pale stripe
Egg case carried by female until hatch
habitatNotes: Allergens and pathogen carriage. Feeds on virtually any organic residue.
management: Gel baits, IGRs, sanitation. Monitor with sticky traps in corners along walls.

quickId: Reddish-brown with pale yellow pronotal margin
Adults fly short distances in warm weather
habitatNotes: Indicator of plumbing or drainage issues. Strong musty odor in heavy infestations.
management: Seal floor drains and sewer access. Bait stations in utility chases.

quickId: Females wingless; males with short wings
Strong preference for damp, cool spaces
habitatNotes: Frequent in basements and crawlspaces; signals moisture problems.
management: Dehumidify, exclude. Granular baits in voids and along foundations.
